questions.objective_ref: Create tuples by packing values (the comma, not the parentheses, makes a tuple).
Create tuples: the single-element trailing-comma gotcha, (42) vs (42,).
Create tuples via the tuple() constructor from an iterable such as a string.
Access elements by indexing/slicing; slicing a tuple returns a new tuple.
Explain immutability: item assignment on a tuple raises TypeError.
Explain immutability: identify what a tuple forbids that a list allows (no append).
Apply tuple unpacking to swap two variables with a, b = b, a (no temp).
Apply tuple unpacking to loop over a list of tuples.
Use nested tuples and reach values with chained indexing.
Perform basic tuple operations: repetition with *.
Use the two tuple methods count() and index().
Perform lexicographic comparison of tuples, element by element.
